mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative in more than 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates immediate capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating short-term positive price pressure on S&P 500 equities within the next trading week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge with p-values exceeding 0.10 in recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 225.0 trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of high-inflation periods, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are fully anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate earnings growth, which immediately supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias relative to GDP levels, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 90.0 indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply-side weakness, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of contraction periods, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly with a KS test p-value above 0.05.
